insight: Doomed Mech with 4 lifespan Fabrication is break-even or bad; 5 lifespan is where it becomes worth targeting.
author: extratricky
context: Discussion of whether to target Fabrication in a breachproof defense set.

insight: In breachproof sets, the general priority for defense allocation is Trinity before attackers, with very rare exceptions related to supply constraints.
author: extratricky
context: Microplay advice for defending against breachproof strategies.

insight: DD/DDE opening should not be used as first player without good reason; requires either a big absorber (more than 3 HP Wall) or specific high-value units like Odin or Lucina in the set.
author: shadourow
context: Player asking about general P1 opening strategy.

insight: Economy hierarchy in opening turns: absorb economy > attack economy > drone economy. However, attack economy should be prioritized early and often due to limited absorb unit availability.
author: masn6811
context: General P1 opening discussion and economy prioritization.

insight: DD/DD followed by DDA is the most standard P1 opening in general cases, developing attack economy on turn 3.
author: shadourow
context: Advice on general P1 opening strategy versus high-econ play.

insight: DD/DTh opening is correct in approximately 90% of Thorium sets.
author: masn6811
context: Specific opening advice for sets containing Thorium.

insight: Cynestra is more practically efficient than Shadowfang due to lower tech cost, though Shadowfang has comparable or better efficiency on paper and is better as a primary attacker when you can mass-produce it.
author: consensus
context: Discussion of unit efficiency between two red attackers.

insight: Shadowfang is preferable to Cynestra in high-econ sets where you can conveniently get a third Red unit, or in mid-econ games with red fixing (Mobile Animus, Flame Animus, Chrono Filter, Blood Phage, Corpus).
author: consensus
context: When Shadowfang becomes viable over Cynestra.

insight: Militia is strong as a first attacker in breachproof sets and with sync attackers like Iso Kronus, and can deny absorb early. However, massing Militia will lose to massed Cynestra.
author: consensus
context: Discussion of when Militia is viable versus when Cynestra outperforms it.

insight: Ice Blade Golem is countered by reaching exactly 6 gold worth of defense as quickly as possible, or by building breachproof. At 6 defense, you start breaking their attack and denying absorb.
author: swapgo
context: Advice on defending against Ice Blade Golem strategies.

insight: Ice Blade Golems counter blockers with even health like Infusion Grid, and are especially good against Doomed Wall.
author: swapgo
context: Ice Blade Golem matchup discussion.

insight: Ice Blade Golems are good counters to Plexo Cell.
author: liadahlia
context: Matchup discussion between Ice Blade Golem and Plexo Cell.

insight: New Thermite (post-2018 balance patch) is overpowered due to spawning 2 Pixies per activation, enabling absorb denial up to 9 attack without sacrificing versatility.
author: consensus
context: Extended discussion of Thermite balance and versatility.

insight: When calculating absorb and soak in complex defensive positions, count down from the defense amount rather than up, or use the n=kill rule: if you attack for opponent's defense minus n, they must sacrifice all units with n health except absorbers.
author: consensus
context: Discussion of mental math techniques for calculating defense outcomes.

insight: Savior should not be built on turn 1 (it costs 6 drones, leaving no harvesting). Default timing is turn 4 for P1 (DD/DDE/DDD/DD1) or either side for P2 (DD/DDE/DDD/DDDE1).
author: consensus
context: When to build Savior and optimal timing.

insight: Optimal Savior timing is determined by whether you can build Blastforge next turn if opponent plays Animus, not by absolute turn number.
author: liadahlia
context: Strategic decision-making around Savior purchases.

insight: Polywall is the best unit to pair with Savior because it provides soak while you wait for Savior to come online, unlike Defense Grid which is a win-more card.
author: liadahlia
context: Discussion of defensive support units in Savior strategies.

insight: Building Animus without sufficient plan to use its energy production (e.g., buying only 2 Perforators from a single Animus) is inefficient; invest in attack economy that can consume the Animus production.
author: amalloy
context: Critique of underutilized Animus purchases.

insight: Absorb denial by early-game attackers (Rager, Lancetooth) is a powerful strategy because Sword (attack value) costs almost nothing before exceeding opponent's absorb.
author: liadahlia
context: Why early absorb denial is effective.

insight: P1 should not go DD/DD/DDEC (double conduit before third turn); DD/DDE/DDD/DDDC is preferred if committing to high economy, since it gives opponent fewer opportunities to punish over-commitment.
author: consensus
context: Debate about specific high-economy opening orders.

insight: In sets with big red support units (Mobile Animus, Flame Animus, Chrono Filter), you may open DD/DD and keep options open for green development on turn 3, sacrificing one green most of the time to maintain flexibility.
author: amalloy
context: Situational opening flexibility in specific set compositions.

insight: You should not assume a strategy is weak or a set is 'basically base set' when you lose to it; instead, analyze why the units worked against you to improve play.
author: leary93
context: Advice on post-game analysis and learning from losses.

insight: Omega Splitter is sufficient justification for opening DD/DDE (high economy with third engineer) as P1.
author: masn6811
context: When third engineer opening becomes viable.

insight: In sets with aggressive units like both Grimbotch and Bloodrager, you should cut drones hard, sometimes as early as DD/DD/Animus with super-aggressive follow-up.
author: masn6811
context: Aggressive opening advice for high-threat sets.

insight: You should generally not build DD/DD/11 (drone rush into Conduit on turn 3); DD/D1 is preferable unless facing a very specific aggressive red rush threat.
author: namington
context: Whether to buy early Conduit or delay economy.

insight: Redeemer's main weakness is vulnerability to burst damage on the turn charges come in; it is stronger with good soak (Aegis) and weaker against burst attackers like Nivo Charge.
author: 307th
context: Strategy advice for playing Redeemer and against it.

insight: Arkavor is both a strong absorber and attacker; it can both slow a game down and speed it up. You should read a set based on Arkavor's role rather than assuming Arkavor defines the set.
author: leary93
context: Strategic flexibility of Arkavor in different contexts.

insight: Fast attack enables Arkavor strategies by providing quick access to 7 attack threshold, unlike most big units which require defensive support.
author: liadahlia
context: Synergy between fast attack and Arkavor availability.
